sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Mr. Jackson, a 45-year-old male, presents with a 3-week history of right elbow pain, which worsened with movement and relieved with rest.
He describes the pain as sharp, stabbing, and aching, and rates it as an 8/10 when using his arm and 3/10 when at rest.
The pain is localized to the outside part of his elbow.
He denies any trauma or recent falls.
He has tried taking Tylenol and ibuprofen, but they provide only temporary relief.
He has been playing tennis more frequently, which he believes may be contributing to the pain.
Past medical history: Mr. Jackson has a history of diabetes type 2 and mild intermittent asthma.
He is taking metformin 500mg twice a day and has an allergy to penicillin.
He has had a surgical history of gallbladder removal 10 years ago.
Review of systems: Mr. Jackson denies any fever or other symptoms related to his elbow pain.
Current medications:
Metformin 500mg twice a day for diabetes
Flovent twice a day for asthma
Albuterol as needed for asthma
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has a grade 3/6 systolic ejection murmur, moderate lateral epicondylar tenderness of the right elbow, and mild pain with resisted extension of the right wrist.
There is no palpable joint effusion.
Test Results:
Right elbow x-ray: No acute fracture or other bony abnormality, no malalignment or sign of joint effusion.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has acute lateral epicondylitis of the right elbow, also known as tennis elbow, likely due to overuse from increased tennis activity.
Problem list:
Lateral epicondylitis of the right elbow, active; diabetes type 2, uncontrolled; mild intermittent asthma, under good control.
Plan:
I prescribed Ultram 50mg once per day for pain management.
I recommend resting the right arm, avoiding repetitive stress, and icing the right elbow for 20 minutes three times a day.
I also ordered a hemoglobin A1c level, CBC blood test, and chem-12 blood test to assess his diabetes control.
I will increase his metformin to 1000mg twice a day.
I will write a prescription for a refill of albuterol.
Follow-up: I will see Mr. Jackson in four weeks to reevaluate his elbow and adjust his treatment plan as needed.
